# Plan cross-domain analytics and reporting

## Summary

Define how ocent should analyze and present information across domains and how each area exposes its own dashboard.

## Why

Tracking data is useful, but one of the main product goals is to help users understand and evaluate their private administrative and financial situation.

## Scope

- define dashboard goals for finance, documents, storage, and contracts
- define initial reporting goals for finance
- define finance KPIs such as XIRR, TTWROR, absolute delta, and relative delta
- define pension insurance KPIs such as XIRR, TTWROR, absolute delta, and relative delta
- define depot KPIs such as dividend yield and dividend income development
- define retirement dashboard requirements including estimated pension, guaranteed pension, projected dividends, and projected interest
- define consolidated KPI views across money accounts, depots, pension insurance records, liabilities, receivables, and real estate
- define net worth views and net worth development logic
- define liquidity forecast requirements based on expected inflows and outflows
- define variance analysis between expected and actual values
- define budgeting requirements for months, years, categories, and savings goals
- define budget versus actual analysis views
- define tax-process reporting requirements such as completeness and review status
- define search-oriented retrieval and discovery expectations across domains
- define valuation logic requirements for non-trivial assets and obligations
- define how containers contribute to yearly or case-based analysis
- identify cross-domain analytics opportunities between documents and finance
- outline future dashboards and review workflows

## Acceptance Criteria

- each major domain has a documented dashboard objective
- analytics goals are documented for the first product phases
- finance and depot KPI definitions are explicitly listed
- pension insurance KPI definitions are explicitly listed
- retirement dashboard requirements are explicitly listed
- consolidated finance dashboard requirements are explicitly listed
- net worth and liquidity forecast requirements are explicitly listed
- variance analysis requirements are explicitly listed
- budgeting and planning requirements are explicitly listed
- tax-process reporting requirements are explicitly listed
- valuation logic requirements are explicitly listed
- at least one yearly container use case is specified
- reporting dependencies on other epics are explicit
- dashboard and analysis follow-up issues can be derived from the output
